serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of raw pistachios is approximately 30g (about 49 kernels or one small handful), providing roughly 168 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Pistachio nuts, raw — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 12652)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (data_source: hpb, confidence 0.95). Cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pistachio nuts, raw (FDC ID 170184) for saturated fat, MUFA, PUFA, trans fat, and micronutrient detail. Values are consistent between both approved sources.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pistachio nuts have a published GI of approximately 15 (low) per the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables published in Diabetes Care. GL per 30g serving calculated as: (15 × 27.17 × 0.30) / 100 = 1.22, rounded to 1.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (1mg/100g) and exceptionally high potassium (1025mg/100g) strongly support blood pressure management
hypertension.guidance: Raw pistachios are highly suitable for hypertension management due to near-zero sodium and one of the highest potassium densities among whole foods (1025mg/100g), directly supporting DASH diet targets; avoid salted pistachio products which can contain 200–500mg sodium/100g, and limit portions to 30–40g due to caloric density.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~15), high dietary fibre (10.6g/100g), and high protein (20.2g/100g) significantly blunt postprandial glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw pistachios are suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management given their low glycaemic index of approximately 15, high fibre content of 10.6g/100g, and high protein of 20.2g/100g which collectively attenuate postprandial glucose rise; portion to 30g per serving and avoid sweetened or honey-roasted pistachio products.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: MUFA/PUFA-dominant fat profile (37g/100g combined), high dietary fibre, zero trans fat, and rich antioxidant content (vitamin E, lutein, zeaxanthin) support cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw pistachios are cardioprotective: their fat profile is dominated by monounsaturated (23.3g/100g) and polyunsaturated (13.7g/100g) fatty acids with only 5.9g/100g saturated fat and zero trans fat, and their high fibre (10.6g/100g), potassium (1025mg/100g), and antioxidant content (vitamin E 2.86mg, lutein/zeaxanthin) collectively support LDL reduction and vascular health; consume as unsalted raw nuts in 30g portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: MUFA/PUFA-dominant whole nut with anti-inflammatory polyphenols, vitamin E, magnesium, and zero trans fat qualifies for the MUFA/PUFA clinical carve-out
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw pistachios are suitable for rheumatoid arthritis as a MUFA/PUFA-dominant whole nut with anti-inflammatory properties from vitamin E (2.86mg/100g), polyphenols (lutein, zeaxanthin), magnesium (121mg/100g), and high fibre; choose unsalted raw pistachios to avoid sodium-driven tissue swelling and introduce gradually to monitor individual sensitivity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pistachio, raw refers to the unprocessed, unshelled or shelled kernel of Pistacia vera, a drupe native to the Middle East and Central Asia. Raw pistachios are light green in colour with a firm, crunchy texture and a characteristically sweet, nutty flavour. They are consumed as a snack, used in confectionery, desserts, and savoury dishes across Middle Eastern, Mediterranean, South Asian, and global cuisines. Nutritionally, raw pistachios are energy-dense at 560 kcal/100g and are distinguished by their exceptional macronutrient balance: 20.2g protein, 45.3g fat (predominantly MUFA at 23.3g and PUFA at 13.7g, with only 5.9g saturated fat and zero trans fat), and 27.2g carbohydrate with 10.6g dietary fibre. Their glycaemic index is approximately 15 (low), making them one of the lowest-GI whole foods available. Potassium content is exceptionally high at 1025mg/100g, placing pistachios among the best dietary potassium sources and strongly aligned with DASH diet principles for hypertension management. Sodium is negligible at 1mg/100g in raw form. Pistachios are also rich in vitamin B6 (1.7mg/100g), phosphorus (490mg/100g), magnesium (121mg/100g), vitamin E (2.86mg/100g), and antioxidant carotenoids including lutein and zeaxanthin. Clinically, raw pistachios are suitable for Type 2 Diabetes (low GI, high fibre, high protein), hypertension (high potassium, negligible sodium), cardiovascular disease (MUFA/PUFA-dominant fat, high fibre, antioxidants), and rheumatoid arthritis (anti-inflammatory nutrient profile). They are naturally vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, halal, and kosher. The primary allergen concern is tree nuts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in raw pistachios?
What is the GI of pistachios?
Are pistachios good for blood pressure?
What are the health benefits of pistachios?

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95. Cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170184) for fatty acid and micronutrient detail. GI value of 15 is published in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables in Diabetes Care. All values are consistent between approved sources.
notes: Saturated fat (5.9g/100g), MUFA (23.26g/100g), PUFA (13.74g/100g), trans fat (0.0g/100g), and detailed micronutrient values (vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 170184 as HPB does not report these fields; values are consistent with HPB primary macros. Cholesterol is confirmed zero per both HPB and USDA sources — pistachios are a plant food and naturally contain no cholesterol. Vitamin B12 and vitamin D are 0.0 as pistachios are a plant food and naturally contain none of these nutrients. The dietary tag 'wholegrain' in the input has been removed as pistachios are tree nuts, not grains. No -1 sentinel values remain undocumented: all -1 defaults from the template have been replaced with sourced values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. GL per 30g serving = (GI 15 × available carbohydrate 27.17g × 0.30) / 100 = 1.22, rounded to 1.2.
